commit.message:
perf: optimize bootstrap for instant data display

1. Parallel probes: terrain + tile probes now run via Promise.all()
   instead of sequential await — saves ~100-200ms on globe init.

2. Hedged fetch: earthquake API gets 3s head start; if slow, USGS
   fetch starts in parallel and Promise.any() races both sources.
   Eliminates worst-case 10s+ sequential timeout.

3. Reorder bootstrap: orchestrators wire before UI mounts, so data
   arriving during DOM creation flows through immediately.

4. Lazy vs30 grid: 500-800KB grid deferred to first GMPE computation
   instead of blocking boot. Preloaded in background.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fetch YouTube monthly VOD stats via RSS + Videos API

now fetches per-video durations and views for the current month using the
quota-efficient RSS + Videos API approach (~2 units per channel).

fix(executor): merge user-selected skills into bot_config for download (#673)

bot_config["skills"] only contained Ghost-level skills, causing the
Executor's download_and_deploy_skills() to skip user-selected personal
skills. Merge all resolved skills into bot_config before constructing
the ExecutionRequest so the Executor downloads them.

commit.message:
fix: retry failed submissions in analyzer result buffer

The submitResultsFunc was not checking for failed submissions from the
server. When all results failed (e.g., photo IDs not found in database),
the function still returned nil (success), causing the buffer to clear
the data and lose the analysis results.

Now the function:
1. Logs the number of failed photos in addition to accepted/rejected
2. Returns an error if any photos failed to process
3. This allows the buffer to restore the data for retry

Also fix backend/.gitignore to use /relive-analyzer to only ignore
the binary in root, not the cmd/relive-analyzer source directory.

commit.message:
fix: VPN client outbound routing — disable rp_filter during active tunnel

LAN device traffic was being silently dropped when routed through the
wg-client VPN tunnel because strict reverse path filtering (rp_filter=1)
rejected return packets arriving on the wg-client interface.

- setup_vpn_client_routing: set rp_filter=0 on all, br0, eth0, eth1,
  and wg-client interfaces so return traffic is accepted
- teardown_vpn_client_routing: restore rp_filter defaults (all=1,
  br0/eth0/eth1=2) when VPN client is deactivated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(ui): consolidate DRY violations + redesign TrayPopup

- Add TASK_ACTIVE_EXCLUSIONS to db_utils as single source of truth
- Replace 7x hardcoded exclusion lists across 5 files with constant
- Use build_priority_order_sql() in get_suggested_tasks + daily_digest
- Use priority_sort_key() from db_utils in FullWindow._sort_tasks
- Remove dead code: search_tasks(), get_overdue(), get_tasks()
  → replaced by get_all_active() + Python-side section filtering
- FullWindow.refresh: 8 queries → 3 (all_active + done + suggested)
- get_summary() accepts pre-fetched tasks to avoid redundant query
- Move purge_old_done from every-30s refresh to startup + hourly timer
- Extract _REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS and _PURGE_INTERVAL_MS constants
- TrayPopup: replace quick-add with collapsible add-form (title,
  description, due_date, priority) via "+" button in header

commit.message:
Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
refactor!: nest Lambda settings under `lambda` key in all handler configs

BREAKING CHANGE: Lambda-specific properties (memory, timeout, logLevel,
permissions) are no longer top-level in handler options. They must now be
passed inside a `lambda: { ... }` object. The global config property
`defaults` is renamed to `lambda`.

Before:
  defineFifoQueue({ memory: 512, timeout: '1m', delay: '2s' })
  defineConfig({ defaults: { memory: 256 } })

After:
  defineFifoQueue({ lambda: { memory: 512, timeout: '1m' }, delay: '2s' })
  defineConfig({ lambda: { memory: 256 } })

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 11.5 — Embed fallback for playlist tracks + backfill tool (#38)

* feat: Phase 11.5 — Embed fallback for playlist tracks + backfill tool

Solve Spotify Development Mode 403 restriction on playlist track endpoints
by using Spotify's embed page as a fallback. Add memory.backfill_playlist
tool for importing existing playlists into the memory ledger.

New features:
- SpotifyEmbedClient: OOP class with configurable timeout/rate limit,
  parses __NEXT_DATA__ from /embed/playlist/{id} for track data
- spotify.get_playlist: embed fallback on 403, tracks_source field
- memory.backfill_playlist: single-call import of existing playlists
- Admin cache invalidation endpoints (playlists + all)
- 23 new tests (embed, backfill, cache admin)
- ChatGPT docs updated (34 tools total)

Code quality:
- EmbedTrackItem moved to models.py as Pydantic BaseModel
- Embed constants centralized in constants.py
- _reconstruct_track_list moved into PlaylistLedgerToolHandlers class
- Fixed all 46 pre-existing mypy errors across the test suite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit PR review — embed retry, migration, schema hardening

- Add Alembic migration 008 for 'backfill' enum value in playlist_snapshot_source
- Add exponential backoff with jitter for embed client 429/5xx responses
- Harden playlist_id schema (min_length=1) + use `is not None` check
- Fix /cache/all/invalidate response message to reflect actual scope
- Add EmbedTrackItem field constraints (min_length, ge=0)
- Log SpotifyEmbedError details in embed fallback warning
- Remove unnecessary outer patch in backfill test
- Add late-import comment for circular dependency clarity
- Update plan-v2.md to match shipped SpotifyEmbedClient API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address second CodeRabbit review — user scoping, embed concurrency, retry safety

- Scope idempotency_key and playlist_id lookups to user_id in backfill (prevents cross-user data leakage)
- Add asyncio.Lock to SpotifyEmbedClient for concurrent request serialization
- Extract _compute_retry_delay method with safe Retry-After header parsing (catches ValueError)
- Add cross-user isolation regression test for backfill idempotency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: replace Spin fallbacks with null/skeleton to fix layout during load

Lazy Drawer/Modal components (HistoryPanel, KeyboardShortcutsPanel,
PreviewModal, PerformancePanel) were using LoadingSpinner as Suspense
fallback. Since these components start closed, the Spin placeholder
was rendered directly in the main layout at 200px height each,
causing 3 visible spinners cluttering the canvas area during load.

- Drawers/Modals: change fallback to null (closed by default, no
  placeholder needed in layout)
- LazyPropertyPanel: change fallback to PropertyPanelSkeleton (always
  visible in the right sider, needs a proper placeholder)
- Remove LoadingSpinner component and its Spin/antd import entirely

Fix: timing bug — store detected IPs on controller before signaling exists

autoDetectNetworkConfig() runs before new CallSignaling() in init(), so
this.signaling was null when trying to set myIp4/myIp6.
Fix: store as this._detectedIp4/6 on PhoneController, apply to signaling
immediately after it's constructed.

Also guard encodeCallAttributes against empty senderIp (defaults to 0.0.0.0)
to prevent undefined bytes crashing the map() call.

fix: corrigir timezone UTC→America/Sao_Paulo em lembretes recorrentes

Node.js rodava em UTC então setHours/toISOString retornavam horário UTC,
causando lembretes às 07:00 SP dispararem às 04:00 SP (07:00 UTC).

- calcularProximaOcorrenciaRecorrente: reescrito com Intl.DateTimeFormat SP
  + ISO strings com offset explícito -03:00
- SQL: LOCALTIME/CURRENT_DATE → NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'America/Sao_Paulo'
- sweeper/worker/handlers: jobId e guard de idempotência usam data SP

Fix: skip build job when demo matrix is empty (#3736)

* fix: skip build job when iOS demo matrix is empty

When a PR has no changed files in demos/ios/, the generate-matrix job
outputs an empty array which causes GitHub Actions to error with
"Matrix vector 'demo' does not contain any values". Adding an `if`
condition skips the build job entirely in that case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip build job when Android demo matrix is empty

Same issue as the iOS workflow: when a PR has no changed files in
demos/android/, the empty matrix causes "Matrix vector 'demo' does not
contain any values". Adding an `if` condition skips the build job.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
